subject: failed novelists
one_liner: The regulated exchange for manuscripts that will never be read.
brand_voice_notes: Deadpan institutional finance. Slush treats abandoned drafts, query letters, and rejection slips as tradeable securities, and speaks about them with the total seriousness of a clearing house. Never acknowledge the joke. Use the vocabulary of exchanges, settlement, custody, and yield. Failed novelists are addressed as sophisticated market participants. The bit holds because nobody on the page finds it strange.

hero.h1: Your unfinished novel is an asset class.
hero.sub: Slush is the regulated exchange for manuscripts that will never be read. List your draft. Short your writing group. Earn yield on the rejection letters you were keeping anyway.

features.title: Manuscript futures
features.blurb: List a work-in-progress as a tradeable contract and let the order book price it. The market sets a number on your fourth draft so you no longer have to.

features.title: Short your cohort
features.blurb: Open a position against any debut listed on the platform. When their agent stops returning emails, your contract settles in the green.

features.title: Rejection staking
features.blurb: Lock your form rejections in custody and collect yield. Eighty rejections clears the high-APY tier. You were filing them in a shoebox regardless.

pricing_tiers.name: Querying
pricing_tiers.tagline: For the writer between drafts and between agents.
perks: Spot trading on your own manuscript

pricing_tiers.name: On Submission
pricing_tiers.tagline: For the writer whose book has been with editors for fourteen months.
perks: Priority settlement the day they get dropped

pricing_tiers.name: Posthumous
pricing_tiers.tagline: Estate-grade liquidity. Billed annually, in advance, to whoever is left.
perks: Trade the back catalog you never wrote
Cold storage for drafts in perpetuity
A dedicated account manager who has read none of it

testimonials.quote: I have been on submission since 2021. Slush is the only platform that let me realize the loss.
testimonials.name: Gerald Pflum
testimonials.title: Author of three unpublished manuscripts and one published acknowledgments page

testimonials.quote: Shorted my entire workshop. Closed up forty percent the week the favorite signed with a small press nobody had heard of.
testimonials.name: Diane Oresky
testimonials.title: MFA fiction, 2019 cohort, did not place

testimonials.quote: My rejection yield now exceeds my royalty income. My royalty income is zero.
testimonials.name: Marcus Heel
testimonials.title: Self-described novelist, employed full time in regional logistics

demo_screen_concept: A serious-looking exchange terminal called the Slush Trading Desk. A left-rail watchlist of manuscript tickers ($WIP, $DRAFT4, $MEMOIR, $NANO, $TRILOGY, $QUERY) each with a last price, daily change percent, and a tiny inline sparkline; a filter box and category tabs (All / Open / Held) above it. A center column with the selected ticker's header (price, change, a lightweight bar chart of recent sessions), a two-sided order book (bids in green, asks in red), and an order ticket where you pick Buy/Sell, market or limit, and a quantity, then place a mock order that appends to an order-history log with a fake fill price. A right rail with a portfolio of held positions (your half-finished novels and their unrealized P/L) and a Rejection Staking panel: a slider for how many rejection letters to lock, which updates a displayed APY and projected monthly yield. Everything is client-side fake data; placing orders and moving the slider mutate the on-screen numbers.

design_direction.style: Swiss Modernism 2.0 — institutional international-typographic exchange. Strict 12-column grid, hairline rules, generous whitespace, zero border-radius, no drop shadows, a single oxblood accent doing all the emphasis. Reads like the brand of a 90-year-old clearing house that happens to clear unfinished fiction.
design_direction.archetype: Institutional financial exchange / securities marketplace landing (prospectus-grade trust signals, deadpan corporate composition)
design_direction.motion_vocab: Restrained. 150-220ms ease-out on hover underlines and ticker color shifts, a subtle marquee ticker tape in the header, no parallax, prefers-reduced-motion respected.
design_direction.hero_composition_notes: Asymmetric Swiss split: oversized Archivo headline pinned to a left baseline grid, a thin live ticker-tape strip across the very top, a compact 'index quote' card on the right showing a fictional SLUSH Composite Index figure and change. Hairline column rules visible. Serif Newsreader sub and fine print read like a securities disclosure. Monospace (Space Mono) reserved for tickers, prices, and figures.
